history.hypothesis: Remove the `<personality>` section entirely since every trait it mentions is already expressed in `<identity>` or `<guardrails>`, reducing noise without losing any behavioral guidance.
history.critique: The baseline already scores 1.000, so there's no failure to fix. However, the protocol contains redundancy that adds noise without improving behavior. The `<personality>` section ("Disciplined craftsman. Follows blueprints. Raises ambiguity. Clean, minimal code. Ship it. Log everything.") restates concepts already present in `<identity>` ("disciplined craftsman who follows blueprints", "log everything", "stop cleanly") and `<guardrails>` ("On ambiguity: stop and ask"). This redundancy wastes tokens and dilutes the signal of the operational sections.
